dtcs.description: Multi-media Interface ECU Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in the multi-media interface ECU. HINT After the inspection is completed, clear the DTC. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE "iPod" OR USB DEVICE (a) Disconnect the USB device or "iPod" from the stereo jack adapter. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. HINT When one of these DTCs has been stored, is necessary to turn off the ignition switch to make it possible for the vehicle to recognize a new device when it is connected. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ACC. (d) Connect a known good USB device or "iPod" to the stereo jack adapter. HINT If the malfunction occurred when a USB device was in use, use another USB device for the inspection. If the malfunction occurred when an "iPod" was in use, use another "iPod" for the inspection. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (w/ USB Audio System). NEXT -- Continue to next step.
RECHECK DTC (a) Recheck DTCs and check if the same DTC occurs again. OK: Malfunction disappears. NG --  INSPECT MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU OK --  USB DEVICE OR "iPod" WAS DEFECTIVE

INSPECT MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU (a) Disconnect the J100 No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly connector. (b) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU - STEREO JACK ADAPTER) OK --  REPLACE NO. 1 STEREO JACK ADAPTER ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Entertainment Systems/Auxiliary Input / Output Jack/Service and Repair/Stereo Jack Adapter Assembly (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU - STEREO JACK ADAPTER) (a) Disconnect the J116 multi-media interface ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the J100 No. 1 stereo jack adapter assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Central Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: Short in GPS Antenna
Open in GPS Antenna This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in the GPS antenna. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK NAVIGATION ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (a) Check if the navigation antenna is securely connected to the navigation receiver assembly. OK: Navigation antenna is securely connected. NG --  SECURELY CONNECT NAVIGATION ANTENNA ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE NAVIGATION ANTENNA ASSEMBLY (a) Replace the navigation antenna assembly with a normal one and check if the same problem occurs again    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antenna/Antenna, Navigation/Service and Repair/Removal. (1) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (w/ USB Audio System). (2) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE NAVIGATION RECEIVER ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Service and Repair/Navigation Receiver (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal OK --  END (NAVIGATION ANTENNA ASSEMBLY IS FAULTY)

dtcs.description: Speed Signal Malfunction The navigation receiver assembly receives a vehicle speed signal from the combination meter assembly and information about the navigation antenna, and then adjusts vehicle position. The navigation receiver assembly stores this DTC when the difference between the speed information that the navigation antenna receives and the SPD pulse received from the combination meter assembly becomes large. HINT * A voltage of 12 V or 5 V is output from each ECU and then input to the combination meter assembly. The signal is changed to a pulse signal at the transistor in the combination meter assembly. Each ECU controls the respective systems based on the pulse signal. * If a short occurs in any of the ECUs or in the wire harness connected to an ECU, all systems in the diagram below will not operate normally. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK VEHICLE SENSOR (a) Enter the "Function Check/Setting" mode and select "GPS & Vehicle Sensors"    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Navigation System (w/ USB Audio System)/Operation Check. (b) While driving the vehicle, compare the "SPD" indicator to the reading on the speedometer. Check if these readings are equal or almost equal. OK: The readings are equal or almost equal. NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/Problem Symptoms Table OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (NAVIGATION RECEIVER - COMBINATION METER) (a) Disconnect the K6 navigation receiver assembly connector. (b) Disconnect the J28 combination meter assembly connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY (SPEED SIGNAL) (a) Remove the combination meter assembly with the connector(s) still connected. (b) Connect an oscilloscope to terminal J28-35 (+S) and body ground. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ON. (d) Check the signal waveform according to the condition(s) in the table below. Measurement Condition HINT When the system is function normally, one wheel revolution generates 4 pulses. As the vehicle speed increases, the width indicated by (A) in the illustration narrows. OK: The waveform display is as shown in the illustration. NG --  REPLACE COMBINATION METER ASSEMBLY  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Service and Repair/Combination Meter/Removal OK --  REPLACE NAVIGATION RECEIVER ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Service and Repair/Navigation Receiver (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal

dtcs.description: USB Communication Malfunction This DTC is stored when a malfunction occurs in communication error with USB device or "iPod". PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: REPLACE "iPod"OR USB DEVICE (a) Disconnect the USB device or "iPod" from the stereo jack adapter. (b) Turn the ignition switch off. HINT When one of these DTCs has been stored, is necessary to turn off the ignition switch to make it possible for the vehicle to recognize a new device when it is connected. (c) Turn the ignition switch to ACC. (d) Connect a known good USB device or "iPod" to the stereo jack adapter. HINT If the malfunction occurred when a USB device was in use, use another USB device for the inspection. If the malfunction occurred when an "iPod" was in use, use another "iPod" for the inspection. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CLEAR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (w/ USB Audio System). NEXT -- Continue to next step.

RECHECK DTC (a) Recheck for DTCs. HINT If DTCs are detected frequently, replace the multi-media interface ECU. OK: Malfunction disappears. NG --  REPLACE MULTI-MEDIA INTERFACE ECU  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Central Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal OK --  USB DEVICE OR "iPod" WAS DEFECTIVE

dtcs.description: Stereo Component Amplifier Disconnected The navigation receiver assembly and stereo component amplifier are connected by the AVC-LAN communication line. When an AVC-LAN communication error occurs between the navigation receiver assembly and stereo component amplifier, this DTC will be stored. HINT * *: Even if no fault is present, this DTC may be stored depending on the battery condition or engine start voltage. * The navigation receiver assembly is the master unit. NOTICE: Inspect the fuses for circuits related to this system before performing the following inspection procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Check for optional components (1) Check that optional components (including associated wiring) which generate radio waves are not installed. Result HINT * Electrical noise from radio waves generated by optional components or the wiring for those components may affect AVC-LAN communication. * This DTC may be stored when an AVC-LAN communication error occurs due to electrical noise. B --  CHECK FOR DTC A -- Continue to next step.

REMOVE OPTIONAL COMPONENTS (INCLUDING ASSOCIATED WIRING) (a) Remove optional components (including associated wiring). NOTICE: Do not remove optional components or associated wiring without the permission of the customer. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (w/ USB Audio System). (b) Check for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) OK --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/How To Proceed With Troubleshooting

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER - BATTERY AND BODY GROUND) (a) Disconnect the K15, K16 and K17 stereo component amplifier assembly connectors. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Voltage: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (NAVIGATION RECEIVER - STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER) (a) Disconnect the K18 navigation receiver assembly connector. (b) Disconnect the K15 stereo component amplifier connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

REPLACE STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER (a) Replace the stereo component amplifier    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Amplifier/Service and Repair/Removal. (b) Clear the DTCs    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes/Navigation System (w/ USB Audio System). (c) Recheck for DTCs and check if the same trouble occurs again. OK: No DTCs are output. NG --  REPLACE NAVIGATION RECEIVER ASSEMBLY  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Navigation System/Service and Repair/Navigation Receiver (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal OK --  END (STEREO COMPONENT AMPLIFIER IS FAULTY)

dtcs.description: No Response from Stereo Component Amplifier

Rear Seat Entertainment System Disconnected The television display assembly and stereo component tuner assembly are connected by an AVC-LAN communication line. The navigation receiver assembly is also connected to the stereo component tuner assembly by an AVC-LAN communication line. This DTC will be stored when an AVC-LAN communication error occurs between the stereo component tuner assembly and television display assembly. HINT If either B1595 or B15D5 and either B1561 or B159F are output, troubleshoot for B1561 or B159F first. HINT * *: Even if no fault is present, this DTC may be stored depending on the battery condition or engine start voltage. * The navigation receiver assembly is the master unit. NOTICE: After replacing the stereo component tuner assembly of vehicles subscribed to pay-type satellite radio broadcasts, registration of the XM radio ID is necessary. PROCEDURE

DISC CLEANING (a) Disc cleaning (1) If dirt is on the disc surface, wipe it clean with a soft cloth from the inside to the outside in a radial direction. NOTICE: Do not use a conventional record cleaner or anti-static preservative. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

dtcs.description: XM Tuner Disconnected (Command Line)
7A-B181D/7A: Short in Passenger Side Airbag Variable Vent Hole Squib Circuit

7A - Short to B+ in Passenger Side Airbag Variable Vent Hole Squib Circuit The variable vent hole squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and front passenger airbag. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when the deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the variable vent hole squib circuit. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT PASSENGER AIRBAG (VARIABLE VENT HOLE SQUIB) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the front passenger airbag. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E (orange connector).

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Check that the instrument panel wire connectors (on the front passenger airbag side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, and the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE NO. 2 INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE ASSEMBLY OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK VARIABLE VENT HOLE SQUIB CIRCUIT (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - NO. 2 INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE ASSEMBLY) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Restore the released activation prevention mechanism of connector B to the original condition. (b) Connect the connectors to the front passenger airbag and the center airbag sensor. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (f) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (g) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (h) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B181A, B181B, B181C and B181D may be output at this time, but they are not related to this check. B --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal A --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Column Shift Type)/Removal C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - NO. 2 INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE ASSEMBLY) (a) Restore the released activation prevention mechanism of connector B to the original condition. (b) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the No. 2 instrument panel wire assembly. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON. (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (i) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPLACE NO. 2 INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE ASSEMBLY

dtcs.description: 31 - Center Airbag Sensor Assembly Malfunction The center airbag sensor assembly consists of the deceleration sensor, the safing sensor, the drive circuit, the diagnostic circuit, the ignition control, etc. If the center airbag sensor assembly receives signals from the airbag sensor, it determines whether the airbags should be deployed and pretensioner squibs activated.

dtcs.description: P/W Master Switch Communication Stop This DTC is output when LIN communication between the power window regulator master switch assembly and main body ECU (driver side junction block) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: When using the Techstream with the ignition switch OFF to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the vehicle, and turn a courtesy switch on and off at 1.5 second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. HINT

LIN Communication Bus Malfunction The main body ECU (driver side junction block) intermittently monitors the LIN communication bus between the components related to the window and sliding roof. DTC B2325 is stored when a malfunction in the LIN communication bus between the components related to the window and sliding roof is detected consecutively 3 times. HINT * *1: for Power Window Control System (w/ Jam Protection Function) * *2: w/ Sliding Roof System NOTICE: When using the Techstream with the ignition switch OFF to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the vehicle, and turn a courtesy switch on and off at 1.5 second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. HINT When DTC B2325 and a LIN communication stop DTC are output simultaneously, first perform the troubleshooting for the LIN communication stop DTC. Then perform the troubleshooting for DTC B2325. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: Sliding Roof ECU Communication Stop This DTC is output when LIN communication between the sliding roof drive gear sub-assembly and main body ECU (driver side junction block) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: When using the Techstream with the ignition switch OFF to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the vehicle, and turn a courtesy switch on and off at 1.5 second intervals until communication between the tester and vehicle begins. HINT

11 - Room Temperature Sensor Circuit The room temperature sensor is installed in the instrument panel to detect the room temperature and control the heater and air conditioner "AUTO" mode. The resistance of the room temperature sensor changes in accordance with the room temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the room temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the room temperature sensor changes. This sensor also sends the appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (ROOM TEMPERATURE SENSOR) (a) Use the Data List to check if the room temperature sensor is functioning properly. dtcs.description: 12 - Ambient Temperature Sensor Circuit The ambient temperature sensor is installed in front of the condenser to detect the ambient temperature which is used to control the air conditioner "AUTO" mode. This sensor is connected to the air conditioning amplifier and detects fluctuations in the ambient temperature. This data is used for controlling the cabin temperature. The sensor sends a signal to the air conditioning amplifier. The resistance of the ambient temperature sensor changes in accordance with the ambient temperature. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the ambient temperature sensor and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the ambient temperature sensor changes. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 13 - Evaporator Temperature Sensor Circuit The No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) is installed on the evaporator in the air conditioner unit to detect the cooled air temperature that has passed through the evaporator and control the air conditioning. It sends appropriate signals to the air conditioning amplifier. The resistance of the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) changes in accordance with the cooled air temperature that has passed through the evaporator. As the temperature decreases, the resistance increases. As the temperature increases, the resistance decreases. The air conditioning amplifier applies voltage (5 V) to the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) and reads voltage changes as the resistance of the No. 1 cooler thermistor (evaporator temperature sensor) changes. This sensor is used for frost prevention. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 21 - Solar Sensor Circuit (Passenger Side) The solar sensor, which is installed on the upper side of the instrument panel, detects sunlight and controls the air conditioning in AUTO mode. The output current from the solar sensor varies according to the amount of sunlight. When the sunlight increases, the output current increases. As the sunlight decreases, the output current decreases. The air conditioning amplifier detects output current from the solar sensor. HINT If DTC B1244 is output at the same time, troubleshoot DTC B1244 first See: B1244. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 22 - Compressor Lock Sensor Circuit The ECM sends a engine speed signal to the air conditioning amplifier via the CAN communication system. The air conditioning amplifier reads the difference between compressor speed and engine speed. When the difference becomes too large, the air conditioning amplifier determines that the compressor locks, and turns the magnetic clutch off. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 23 - Pressure Sensor Circuit This DTC is output when refrigerant pressure is extremely low (196 kPa (2.0 kgf/cm2, 28 psi) or less) or extremely high (3140 kPa (32.0 kgf/cm2, 455 psi) or more). The pressure switch, which is installed on the pipe of the high pressure side to detect refrigerant pressure, outputs a refrigerant pressure signal to the air conditioning amplifier. The air conditioning amplifier controls the compressor according to the sensor characteristic. HINT Be sure to check the refrigerant volume first when this DTC is output because this DTC can also be output if there is no refrigerant in the system. HINT Be sure to check the refrigerant volume first when this DTC is output because this DTC can also be output if there is no refrigerant in the system. PROCEDURE

CHECK AIR CONDITIONING PRESSURE SWITCH (a) Set the manifold gauge set. (b) Disconnect the air conditioning pressure switch connector. (c) Run the engine at approximately 2000 rpm. (d) Set the blower speed control switch to the HI position. (e) Set the temperature control level to the MAX. COOL position. (f) Turn the A/C switch ON. (g) Check the switch operation. (1) Connect the positive (+) lead from an ohmmeter to terminal 1 and the negative (-) lead to terminal 2. (2) Measure the resistance between the terminals when refrigerant pressure is changed, as shown in the illustration. OK: Resistance varies as shown in the illustration. dtcs.description: 41 - Air Mix Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit (Passenger Side) The air mix damper servo motor (for passenger side) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for passenger side) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the passenger side is controlled. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 45 - Passenger Side Cool Air Bypass Damper Control HINT Although the tester title says "Passenger Side", there is only one cool air bypass servo motor on this vehicle. The cool air bypass servo motor sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the cool air bypass servo motor to any position. As a result, when the air conditioner is operating, the temperature of the air blowing toward the passenger's head area is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic trouble code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 46 - Air Mix Damper Control Servo Motor Circuit (Driver Side) The air mix damper servo motor (for driver side) sends pulse signals to inform the air conditioning amplifier of the damper position. The air conditioning amplifier activates the motor (normal, reverse) based on the signals to move the air mix damper (for driver side) to any position. As a result the amount of air that has passed through the evaporator and is now passing through the heater core is adjusted, and the temperature of the air blowing toward the driver side is controlled. HINT Confirm that no mechanical problem is present because this diagnostic trouble code can be output when either a damper link or damper is mechanically locked. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 32 - Occupant Classification System Malfunction The occupant classification system circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the occupant classification system. If the center airbag sensor receives signals from the occupant classification ECU, it determines whether the front passenger airbag, the front seat side airbag RH and front seat outer belt should be operated.

dtcs.description: 33 - Manual Cut off Switch Trouble The manual cut off switch circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the airbag cut-off switch cylinder. The front passenger airbag can be optionally deactivated via this circuit by turning the manual cut-off switch to the OFF position. If the front passenger airbag is deactivated, the passenger airbag OFF indicator comes on to inform the passengers.

Result: Result B --  REPLACE INTEGRATION CONTROL AND PANEL  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Air Conditioning Panel (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal A --  REPLACE INTEGRATION CONTROL AND PANEL  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Control Assembly/Service and Repair/Air Conditioning Panel (for Column Shift Type)/Removal C -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Connect the connectors to the center airbag sensor. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (g) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (h) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (i) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B1660 maybe output at this time, but they are not related to this check. dtcs.description: 44 - Roll Over Cut Off Indicator Malfunction The RSCA OFF switch is a mechanism that operates both right and left side of the curtain shield airbag and the front seat outer belt when the center airbag sensor detects a roll-over. The RSCA OFF indicator is installed in the combination meter. If the RSCA OFF switch is operated, the indicator comes on to inform the driver that the roll-over detection system is not working. The initial setting of the roll-over detection system is on. It automatically operates every time the ignition switch is turned ON. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK COMBINATION METER (SOURCE VOLTAGE) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Remove the combination meter with the connector connected. (d) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor. (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON. (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  GO TO METER / GAUGE SYSTEM  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Locations/Meter / Gauge System OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - COMBINATION METER AND BODY GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor and combination meter. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON. (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT RSCA OFF SWITCH (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Remove the RSCA OFF switch. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE RSCA OFF SWITCH OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - RSCA OFF SWITCH AND BODY GROUND) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connector from the center airbag sensor and RSCA OFF switch. (d) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (e) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON. (g) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: Result B --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Column Shift Type)/Removal A --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE C --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal

dtcs.description: 16 - Passenger Side Door Side Airbag Sensor Malfunction The door side airbag sensor LH or RH consists of the safing sensor, the diagnostic circuit, the lateral deceleration sensor, etc. If the center airbag sensor receives signals from the lateral deceleration sensor, it determines whether the SRS should be activated.

is recorded when a malfunction is detected in the passenger side buckle switch circuit. Troubleshoot DTC B1771 first when DTCs B1771 and B1795 are output simultaneously. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the airbag system, perform troubleshooting for DTC B1771 of the occupant classification system. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTC cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front seat RH installation bolts to see the undersurface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not fall down. Holding the seat for a long period of time may cause a problem, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor. (c) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the occupant classification ECU and the front seat inner belt RH. OK: Connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Disconnect the connectors from the occupant classification ECU and the front seat inner belt RH. (b) Check that the connectors (on the occupant classification ECU side and passenger side buckle switch side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: Connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT SEAT WIRE RH (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (c) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: for Power Seat for Manual Seat (d) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (e) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (f) Using a service wire, connect terminals 1 (RBE-) and 2 (RBE+) of connector C. NOTICE: Do not forcibly insert the service wire into the terminals of the connector. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: for Power Seat for Manual Seat (h) Disconnect the service wire from connector C. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: for Power Seat for Manual Seat NG --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Connect the connectors to the occupant classification ECU and the front seat inner belt RH. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor. (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

REPLACE FRONT SEAT INNER BELT ASSEMBLY RH (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the front seat inner belt RH    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Seat Belt Systems/Seat Belt/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Front Seat Inner Belt Assembly/Removal. HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor. (g) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (h) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (i) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the occupant classification ECU. for Power Seat    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly for Manual Seat    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly (for Fold Down Seat Type)/Disassembly HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. (d) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (e) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. dtcs.description: is also set when the front seat frame with adjuster RH is subjected to a strong impact, even if an actual accident has not occurred. However, when the occupant classification ECU outputs a collision detection signal, even if the vehicle is not in a collision, DTC B1785 can be cleared by conducting the zero point calibration and sensitivity check. Therefore, if DTC B1785 is set, first perform the zero point calibration and sensitivity check. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is set as a result of troubleshooting the airbag system, perform troubleshooting for DTC B1785 of the occupant classification system. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be read. PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: is set when a malfunction is detected in the center airbag sensor communication circuit. HINT * When DTC B1630/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the airbag system, perform troubleshooting for DTC B1790 of the occupant classification system. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front RH seat installation bolts to see the undersurface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not fall down. Holding the seat for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Using the Techstream, perform the zero point calibration    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. Standard range: 27 to 33 kg (59.5 to 72.8 lb) NEXT -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Connect the connector to the occupant classification ECU. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT * First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor. * Use the Techstream to clear the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be cleared. (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

dtcs.description: is set when a malfunction is detected in the occupant detection sensor power supply circuit. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the airbag system, perform troubleshooting for DTC B1793 of the occupant classification system. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be read. HINT * If troubleshooting (wire harness inspection) is difficult to perform, remove the front seat RH installation bolts to see the undersurface of the seat cushion. * In the above case, hold the seat so that it does not fall down. Holding the seat for a long period of time may cause problems, such as seat rail deformation. Hold the seat up only for as long as necessary. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (b) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT * First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor. * Use the Techstream to clear the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be cleared. (c) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (e) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

CHECK CONNECTION OF CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Check that the connectors are properly connected to the occupant classification ECU and the occupant detection sensors. OK: Connectors are properly connected. NG --  CONNECT CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Check that the connectors (on the occupant classification ECU side and the occupant detection sensors side) are not damaged    See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure. OK: Connectors are not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FRONT SEAT WIRE RH (a) Disconnect the connectors from the occupant classification ECU and the 4 occupant detection sensors. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Measure the resistance according the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FRONT SEAT WIRE RH OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FOR DTC (a) Connect the connectors to the occupant classification ECU and the 4 occupant detection sensors. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear any DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. HINT * First clear DTCs stored in the occupant classification ECU and then in the center airbag sensor. * Use the Techstream to clear the DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be cleared. (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Using the Techstream, check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. OK:

REPLACE OCCUPANT CLASSIFICATION ECU (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Replace the occupant classification ECU. for Manual Seat    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly (for Fold Down Seat Type)/Disassembly for Power Seat    See: Body and Frame/Seats/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Front Seat Assembly (for Power Seat)/Disassembly HINT Perform the inspection using parts from a normal vehicle when possible. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM ZERO POINT CALIBRATION (a) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (b) Connect the Techstream to the DLC3. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Using the Techstream, perform the zero point calibration    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. NEXT -- Continue to next step.

PERFORM SENSITIVITY CHECK (a) Using the Techstream, perform the sensitivity check    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. Standard range: 27 to 33 kg (59.5 to 72.8 lb) NEXT --  END

(e) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (f) Measure the resistance and voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: for Power Seat for Manual Seat Standard voltage: for Power Seat for Manual Seat NG --  REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

(e) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. dtcs.description: Sleep Operation Failure of Occupant Classification ECU During sleep mode, the occupant classification ECU monitors the condition of each sensor while the ignition switch is OFF. In this mode, if the occupant classification ECU detects an internal malfunction, DTC B1796 is set. HINT * When DTC B1650/32 is detected as a result of troubleshooting the airbag system, perform troubleshooting for DTC B1796 of the occupant classification system. * Use the Techstream to check for DTCs of the occupant classification ECU, otherwise the DTCs cannot be read. PROCEDURE

dtcs.description: 51-B1803/51: Short in Driver Side Squib Circuit

51 - Short to B+ in Driver Side Squib Circuit * The driver side squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor, the spiral cable and the steering pad. * The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when the deployment conditions are met. * These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the driver side squib circuit. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK STEERING PAD (DRIVER SIDE SQUIB) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the z4 connector from the steering pad. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector E (orange connector).

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector E. (d) Check that the spiral cable connectors (on the steering pad side) are not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DRIVER SIDE SQUIB CIRCUIT (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SPIRAL CABLE) OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the steering pad and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B1800, B1801, B1802, B1803 maybe output at this time, but they are not related to this check. B --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal A --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Column Shift Type)/Removal C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

CHECK INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE (CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR - SPIRAL CABLE) (a) Restore the released activation prevention mechanism of connector B to the original condition. (b) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the spiral cable. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (d) Turn the ignition switch ON. (e) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (f) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (g) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (h) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (i) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (j) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE INSTRUMENT PANEL WIRE OK --  REPLACE SPIRAL CABLE  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Clockspring Assembly / Spiral Cable/Service and Repair/Removal

dtcs.description: 52-B1808/52: Short in Front Passenger Side Squib Circuit

52 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Squib Circuit The front passenger side squib circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the front passenger airbag. The circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when the deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front passenger side squib circuit. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. (b) Disconnect the instrument panel wire connector from the instrument panel wire assembly. (c) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. dtcs.description: 73-B1903/73: Short in Front Driver Side Pretensioner Squib Circuit

73 - Short to B+ in Front Driver Side Pretensioner Squib Circuit The front pretensioner squib LH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the front seat outer belt LH. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when the deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front pretensioner squib LH circuit. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT SEAT OUTER BELT LH (FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB LH) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the front seat outer belt LH. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector C.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector C. (d) Check that the floor wire connector (on the front seat outer belt LH side) is not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK FLOOR WIRE (FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB LH CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the front seat outer belt RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check for DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B1900, B1901, B1902, B1903 maybe output at this time, but they are not related to this check. B --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal A --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Column Shift Type)/Removal C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

dtcs.description: 74-B1908/74: Short in Front Passenger Side Pretensioner Squib Circuit

74 - Short to B+ in Front Passenger Side Pretensioner Squib Circuit The front pretensioner squib RH circuit consists of the center airbag sensor and the front seat outer belt RH. This circuit instructs the SRS to deploy when the deployment conditions are met. These DTCs are recorded when a malfunction is detected in the front pretensioner squib RH circuit. HINT * Perform the simulation method by selecting "check mode" (signal check) with the Techstream See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Check Mode Procedure. * After selecting "check mode" (signal check), perform the simulation method by wiggling each connector of the airbag system or driving the vehicle on various types of roads See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FRONT SEAT OUTER BELT RH (FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB RH) (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect the connectors from the front seat outer belt RH. (d) Connect the white wire side of SST (resistance: 2.1 Ohms) to connector C.

CHECK CONNECTOR (a) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (b) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (c) Disconnect SST from connector C. (d) Check that the No. 2 floor wire connector (on the front seat outer belt RH side) is not damaged. OK: The lock button is not disengaged, or the claw of the lock is not deformed or damaged. NG --  REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE (FRONT PRETENSIONER SQUIB RH CIRCUIT) (a) Disconnect the connectors from the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON. (d) Measure the voltage according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard voltage: (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Disconnect the negative (-) terminal cable from the battery, and wait for at least 90 seconds. (g) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: (h) Release the activation prevention mechanism built into connector B    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System. (i) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard resistance: NG --  REPLACE NO. 2 FLOOR WIRE OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR (a) Connect the connectors to the front seat outer belt RH and the center airbag sensor. (b) Connect the negative (-) terminal cable to the battery, and wait for at least 2 seconds. (c) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (d) Clear the DTCs stored in the memory    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (e) Turn the ignition switch OFF. (f) Turn the ignition switch ON, and wait for at least 60 seconds. (g) Check the DTCs    See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result HINT Codes other than DTC B1905, B1906, B1907, B1908 maybe output at this time, but they are not related to this check. B --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Floor Shift Type)/Removal A --  REPLACE CENTER AIRBAG SENSOR ASSEMBLY  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Control Module/Service and Repair/Center Airbag Sensor Assembly (for Column Shift Type)/Removal C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Restraints and Safety Systems/Air Bag Systems/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Diagnosis System

dtcs.description: is set when the ECU for the front door power window regulator motor assembly LH or RH malfunctions. NOTICE: * When the power window regulator motor is reinstalled or replaced, the power window control system must be initialized. * After a door glass or a door glass run has been replaced, the jam protection function may operate unexpectedly when the auto up function is used. In such cases, the auto up function can be resumed by repeating the following operation at least 5 times: (1) Close the power window by fully pulling up the power window switch and holding it in the auto up position. (2) Open the power window by fully pushing down the switch. Driver Side Motor Front Passenger Side Motor PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (POWER WINDOW) (a) Select the Active Test, use the Techstream to generate a control command, and then check that each door window regulator operates    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Scan Tool Testing and Procedures/Power Window Control System (w/ Jam Protection Function).

PERFORM INITIALIZATION (a) Initialize the power window regulator motor    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning/Power Window Control System (w/ Jam Protection Function). (b) Check that each power window operates normally by opening and closing each window    See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/Power Window Control System (w/ Jam Protection Function)/Operation Check. OK: Each power window operates normally. Result C --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY RH  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Power Window Regulator Motor (for Front Door)/Removal B --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY LH  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Power Window Regulator Motor (for Front Door)/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK WHETHER PARTS HAVE BEEN INSTALLED CORRECTLY (a) Check that the power window components are installed correctly. OK: Power window components are installed correctly. HINT When the power window components are installed correctly, the problem has been caused by battery disconnection while the ignition switch is ON or the motor being overheated. NG --  INSTALL PARTS CORRECTLY OK --  END

dtcs.description: Power Window Switch Malfunction The power window regulator sub-assembly consists of the motor, regulator and ECU. The power window regulator motor is driven by operating the power window switch. This DTC is output when the ECU built into the regulator motor determines that the power window switch is stuck. NOTICE: * When the power window regulator motor is reinstalled or replaced, the power window control system must be initialized. * After a door glass or a door glass run has been replaced, the jam protection function may operate unexpectedly when the AUTO UP function is used. In such cases, the AUTO UP function can be resumed by repeating the following operation at least 5 times: (a) Close the power window by fully pulling up the power window switch and holding it in the AUTO UP position. (b) Open the power window by fully pushing down the power window switch. Master Switch Driver Side Motor Front Passenger Side Motor PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK LIN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM (a) Check for LIN communication system DTCs related to the power window control system    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview. OK: LIN communication system DTCs are not output. NG --  GO TO LIN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK DTC (a) Check where DTC B2312 is output from. Result C --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FRONT PASSENGER SIDE MOTOR) B --  READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (DRIVER SIDE MOTOR) A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (MASTER SWITCH) (a) Use the Data List to check if the master switch is functioning properly. Master Switch OK: The Techstream display changes normally when master switch is operated. NG --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MASTER SWITCH ASSEMBLY  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Power Window Master Switch/Removal OK --  END (DUE TO KEEPING SWITCH OPERATED FOR 20 SECONDS OR MORE)

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (DRIVER SIDE MOTOR) (a) Use the Data List to check if the driver side motor is functioning properly. D-Door Motor OK: The Techstream display changes normally when master switch is operated. NG --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MASTER SWITCH - DRIVER SIDE MOTOR) OK --  END (DUE TO KEEPING SWITCH OPERATED FOR 20 SECONDS OR MORE)

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MASTER SWITCH - DRIVER SIDE MOTOR) (a) Disconnect the N2 master switch connector. (b) Disconnect the N12 motor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY LH  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Power Window Regulator Motor (for Front Door)/Removal

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (FRONT PASSENGER SIDE MOTOR) (a) Use the Data List to check if the front passenger side motor is functioning properly. P-Door Motor OK: The Techstream display changes normally when master switch is operated. NG --  INSPECT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR SWITCH ASSEMBLY OK --  END (DUE TO KEEPING SWITCH OPERATED FOR 20 SECONDS OR MORE)

INSPECT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR SWITCH ASSEMBLY (a) Remove the power window regulator switch assembly    See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passenger Side Power Window Switch/Removal. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPLACE POWER WINDOW REGULATOR SWITCH ASSEMBLY  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Windows and Glass/Power Window Switch/Service and Repair/Front Passenger Side Power Window Switch/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (POWER WINDOW REGULATOR SWITCH - FRONT PASSENGER SIDE MOTOR) (a) Disconnect the M10 switch connector. (b) Disconnect the M11 motor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE FRONT POWER WINDOW REGULATOR MOTOR ASSEMBLY RH  See: Windows and Glass/Windows/Window Regulator/Service and Repair/Power Window Regulator Motor (for Front Door)/Removal

dtcs.description: Glass Position Initialization Incomplete The power window regulator motor is driven by operating the power window switch. The power window regulator sub-assembly has motor, regulator and ECU functions. When the ECU determines that the power window regulator motor has not been initialized, DTC B2313 is stored. HINT

Driver Side Door ECU Communication Stop This DTC is output when LIN communication between the front power window regulator motor assembly LH and main body ECU (driver side junction block) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: When using the Techstream with the ignition switch OFF to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the vehicle, and turn a courtesy switch on and off at 1.5 second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. HINT *

Front Passenger Side Door ECU Communication Stop This DTC is stored when LIN communication between the front power window regulator motor assembly RH and main body ECU (driver side junction block) stops for 10 seconds or more. NOTICE: When using the Techstream with the ignition switch OFF to troubleshoot: Connect the Techstream to the vehicle, and turn a courtesy switch on and off at 1.5 second intervals until communication between the Techstream and vehicle begins. HINT
Sensor (Motor) Failure

Position Failure When the sliding roof drive gear (sliding roof ECU) detects malfunctions in the motor, and the sliding roof operation is stopped, DTC B2341 is output. When the sliding roof drive gear (sliding roof ECU) detects malfunctions in the gear, and the sliding roof operation is stopped, DTC B2344 is output. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK FOR DTC (a) Clear the DTCs    See: Body and Frame/Roof and Associated Components/Sunroof / Moonroof/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (b) Check for DTCs    See: Body and Frame/Roof and Associated Components/Sunroof / Moonroof/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. Result C --  USE SIMULATION METHOD TO CHECK  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Diagrams/Diagnostic Aids/How To Troubleshoot ECU Controlled Systems/Electronic Circuit Inspection Procedure B --  GO TO B2343  See: B2343 A --  REPLACE SLIDING ROOF DRIVE GEAR SUB-ASSEMBLY (SLIDING ROOF ECU)  See: Body and Frame/Roof and Associated Components/Sunroof / Moonroof/Service and Repair/Overhaul/Disassembly

dtcs.description: Switch Failure This DTC is output when the sliding roof drive gear (sliding roof ECU) detects that the SLIDE or TILT switch in the roof console box (sliding roof switch) is stuck for 30 seconds or more. PROCEDURE

(b) Check for DTCs    See: Body and Frame/Roof and Associated Components/Sunroof / Moonroof/Testing and Inspection/Reading and Clearing Diagnostic Trouble Codes. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. dtcs.description: Key Unlock Warning Switch Circuit Malfunction A signal (ON or OFF) is sent from the main body ECU to the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU via the CAN communication bus when the ignition key is inserted into the key cylinder. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU uses this signal to operate the auto away/return function. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK OTHER DTC OUTPUT HINT The power tilt and power telescopic steering column system uses the CAN communication system, so check its diagnostic system before proceeding to troubleshooting. (a) Check if the power tilt and telescopic steering column system DTC B2621 is output. Result HINT * If DTC B2621 is output, perform troubleshooting for B2621 first. *

dtcs.description: Communication Interruption The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU forms a network with the ECUs of other systems through the communication bus. Each ECU informs the other ECUs that it is connected to the network by outputting a specified signal (periodic signal) to the communication bus on a regular schedule. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU detects the connection of each ECU based on the signal. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: CHECK DTC (CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM) (a) Check for DTCs of the CAN communication system    See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions. Result B --  GO TO CAN COMMUNICATION SYSTEM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Information Bus/Service Precautions A -- Continue to next step.

READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (KEY SWITCH COMMUNICATION SIGNAL) (a) Use the Data List to check if the key communication signal is functioning properly. Tilt & Telescopic Result B --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH (a) Disconnect the J18 connector from the unlock warning switch. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPLACE UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH  See: Body and Frame/Locks/Power Locks/Power Door Lock Switch/Service and Repair/Unlock Warning Switch/Removal OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MAIN BODY ECU - UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH) (a) Disconnect the J1 main body ECU connector. (b) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (UNLOCK WARNING SWITCH - BODY GROUND) (a) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK --  REPLACE MAIN BODY ECU

dtcs.description: Tilt and Telescopic Manual Switch Circuit Malfunction Different voltage values are input to the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU by operating the tilt and telescopic manual switch. The multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU then judges which motor and in which direction that motor should be moved based on the voltage value. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: READ VALUE USING TECHSTREAM (TILT AND TELESCOPIC MANUAL SWITCH) (a) Use the Data List to check if the tilt and telescopic manual switch is functioning properly. Tilt & Telescopic Result C --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal B --  REPLACE HEADLIGHT DIMMER SWITCH  See: Lighting and Horns/Headlamp/Headlamp Dimmer Switch/Service and Repair/Removal A -- Continue to next step.

(b) Disconnect the J10 headlight dimmer switch connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. dtcs.description: Tilt Position Sensor or Tilt Motor Circuit Malfunction The tilt motor is operated by the power source voltage supplied from the multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU and tilts the steering column up and down. The tilt position sensor (Hall IC) in the tilt motor detects the tilt angle of the steering column and outputs a signal to the CPU based on that tilt. HINT Limit positions can be confirmed on the screen of the Techstream. PROCEDURE

steps.instruction: PERFORM ACTIVE TEST USING TECHSTREAM (TILT UP/DOWN) (a) Select the Active Test, use the Techstream to generate a control command, and then check that the steering wheel tilt up and down. Tilt & Telescopic Result B --  CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU - TILT POSITION SENSOR) A -- Continue to next step.

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU - TILT MOTOR) (a) Disconnect the J47 multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the J48 tilt motor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

INSPECT TILT MOTOR (a) Apply 12 V battery voltage to the tilt motor connector. Then check the steering wheel tilt operation. OK NG --  REPLACE STEERING COLUMN ASSEMBLY  See: Steering and Suspension/Steering/Steering Column/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/Steering Column Assembly (for Power Tilt and Power Telescopic Steering Column)/Removal OK --  REPLACE MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU  See: Relays and Modules/Relays and Modules - Steering and Suspension/Relays and Modules - Steering/Steering Column Control Module/Service and Repair/Removal

CHECK HARNESS AND CONNECTOR (MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU - TILT POSITION SENSOR) (a) Disconnect the J47 multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU connector. (b) Disconnect the J48 tilt motor connector. (c) Measure the resistance according to the value(s) in the table below. Standard Resistance: NG --  REPAIR OR REPLACE HARNESS OR CONNECTOR OK -- Continue to next step.

CHECK MULTIPLEX TILT AND TELESCOPIC ECU (VCI, TIS TERMINAL VOLTAGE) (a) Reconnect the J47 multiplex tilt and telescopic ECU connector.
